parts: I'm a software engineer with 8 years of experience, mostly in backend systems. I've been thinking about transitioning into product management.
That's an interesting transition! What draws you to product management?

I love the technical side but I find myself more energized when I'm talking to users and figuring out what to build. I believe the best products come from deeply understanding user pain points, not from following feature roadmaps.
That's a great philosophy. User empathy is crucial in PM work.
Yeah, and honestly I prefer working at startups over big tech. The pace is better and my work has more visible impact. I've been at Google and Amazon before and felt like a small cog.
I also strongly prefer async communication over meetings. I think most meetings could be emails or docs. Deep work time is sacred to me.
One thing I know for sure is that I want to build something in the health tech space eventually. I'm passionate about mental health tooling — it's underfunded and overlooked.
